name: Mock Test - 04
instruction: The Question Paper contains three sections. Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions. All questions carry equal marks. There is no negative marking.

questions.question_text: A metallic crystal crystallizes into a lattice containing sequence of layers ABABAB... any packing of spheres leaves out voids in the lattice. What percentage by volume of this lattice is empty space?
questions.question_html: A metallic crystal crystallizes into a lattice containing sequence of layers ABABAB... any packing of spheres leaves out voids in the lattice. What percentage by volume of this lattice is empty space?
questions.explanation: 74% space in crystal is filled. Therefore, empty space = (100-74)% = 26%

questions.question_text: Solution showing (+) ve deviation from Raoult’s law includes
questions.question_html: Solution showing (+) ve deviation from Raoult’s law includes
questions.explanation: Acetone + CS 2

questions.question_text: The structure of ClF 3 is
questions.question_html: The structure of ClF3 is
questions.explanation: CN=0.5(V+M-C+A) For. ClF 3 CN= 5 so hybridisation is sp3d. The structure is trigonal bipyramidal. ClF 3 has 10 electrons around the central atom. this means there are 5 electron pairs arranged in a trigonal bipyramidal shape with a 90 0 F-Cl-F bond angle. There are 2 equatorial lone pairs making the final structure T- shaped.

questions.question_text: Isomers of the compound C 4 H 9 Br are:
questions.question_html: Isomers of the compound C4H9Br are:

options.text: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane
options.html: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane

questions.correct_option_text: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane
questions.explanation: Isomers are compounds having the same molecular formula but different structures. In the given straight-chain 4-C compounds, Br is placed at different positions but the molecular formula is same. {tex}\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}CH(Br)C{H_3}}\limits_{2 - Bromobu\tan e} ,\;\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}C{H_2}Br}\limits_{1 - Bromobu\tan e} {/tex} In the given branched 4-C compounds, there is a branching of -CH 3 at carbon 2 but Br is placed at positions 1 and 2 respectively. {tex}\mathop {C{H_3}CH(C{H_3})C{H_2}Br}\limits_{1 - Bromo - 2 - methylpropane} ,\;\mathop {C{H_3}C(Br)(C{H_3})C{H_3}}\limits_{2 - Bromo - 2 - methylpropane} {/tex} So, all of these are isomers since they have the same molecular formula but different structures.

questions.question_text: One of the following alcohols do not undergo oxidation reaction:
questions.question_html: One of the following alcohols do not undergo oxidation reaction:
questions.explanation: The outcome of oxidation reactions of alcohol depends on the substituents on the carbinol carbon. In order for each oxidation step to occur, there must be H on the carbinol carbon. Primary alcohols can be oxidized to aldehydes or further to carboxylic acids. In aqueous media, the carboxylic acid is usually the major product. PCC or PDC, which are used in dichloromethane, allow the oxidation to be stopped at the intermediate aldehyde. Secondary alcohols can be oxidized to ketones but no further: Tertiary alcohols cannot be oxidized (no carbinol C-H).

questions.question_text: Glucose does not give Schiff’s test because of the formation of cyclic ________.
questions.question_html: Glucose does not give Schiff’s test because of the formation of cyclic ________.
questions.explanation: Schiff's test is given by aldehydes. Despite having the aldehyde group, glucose does not give Schiff’s test as aldehydic group is not free here. It was proposed that one of the —OH groups may add to the —CHO group and form a cyclic hemiacetal structure. It was found that glucose forms a six-membered ring in which —OH at C-5 is involved in ring formation. This explains the absence of —CHO group and also existence of glucose in two cyclic hemiacetal forms α-D-(+)-Glucose and β-D-(+)-Glucose.

questions.question_text: Close packing is maximum in the crystal which is a:
questions.question_html: Close packing is maximum in the crystal which is a:
questions.explanation: 74% space in the crystal is filled in face centred cube.

questions.question_text: In which unit, the concentration of solution remains independent of temperature
questions.question_html: In which unit, the concentration of solution remains independent of temperature
questions.explanation: It is independent of volume hence independent of Temperature.

questions.question_text: Which of the following oxides reacts with both HCl and NaOH?
questions.question_html: Which of the following oxides reacts with both HCl and NaOH?
questions.explanation: ZnO is an amphoteric oxide. it reacts with both acid and base. With HCl, it forms zinc chloride and water and with NaOH, it forms sodium zincate and water. ZnO(s) + 2HCl(l) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} ZnCl 2 (s) + H 2 O(l) ZnO(s) + 2NaOH(l) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Na 2 ZnO 2 (s) + H 2 O

questions.question_text: In alkyl halide:
questions.question_html: In alkyl halide:

options.text: the halogen atom bears a partial negative charge
options.html: the halogen atom bears a partial negative charge

options.text: the carbon atom of C-halogen bond bears a partial positive charge
options.html: the carbon atom of C-halogen bond bears a partial positive charge

options.text: the carbon-halogen bond of alkyl halide is polarized
options.html: the carbon-halogen bond of alkyl halide is polarized

questions.explanation: Since halogen atoms are more electronegative than carbon, the carbon-halogen bond of alkyl halide is polarized; the carbon atom bears a partial positive charge whereas the halogen atom bears a partial negative charge.

questions.question_text: The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is lower than that of cyclohexyl chloride because
questions.question_html: The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is lower than that of cyclohexyl chloride because

options.text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene
options.html: Cl atom is linked to sp<sup>2</sup> hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene

options.text: Cl atom is more electropositive in chlorobenzene
options.html: Cl atom is more electropositive in chlorobenzene

options.text: Cl atom is more electronegative in cyclohexyl chloride
options.html: Cl atom is more electronegative in cyclohexyl chloride

options.text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in cyclohexyl chloride
options.html: Cl atom is linked to sp<sup>2</sup> hybridized carbon atom in cyclohexyl chloride

questions.correct_option_text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene
questions.explanation: sp 2 carbon is more electronegative than sp 3 carbon. The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is less than cyclohexyl chloride as in chlorobenzene C of C-Cl bond is sp 2 hybridised where as in cyclohexyl chloride the C of C-Cl bond is sp 3 hybridised. As sp 2 hybridised C has more s-character and is more electronegative than sp 3 hybridised C, the resulting difference in electronegativities of C and Cl in chlorobenzene is less as compared to that in cyclohexyl chloride. So chlorobenzene is less polar than cyclohexyl chloride.

questions.question_text: Nucleic acids are the polymers of
questions.question_html: Nucleic acids are the polymers of
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids are polymers of nucleotides in which nucleic acids are linked together by a phosphodiester linkage also called a polynucleotide. e.g., DNA, RNA, etc.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an amorphous solid? Graphite (C) Quartz glass (SiO 2 ) Chrome alum Silicon carbide (SiC)
questions.question_html:
Which of the following is an amorphous solid?

  1. Graphite (C)
  2. Quartz glass (SiO2 )
  3. Chrome alum
  4. Silicon carbide (SiC)
questions.explanation: Quartz glass is an amorphous or non-crystalline solid because it lacks in a long-range order i.e. a regular long pattern of constituent particles in three dimensions which periodically repeats itself throughout the whole crystal and have a definite geometrical shape which is the case for other 3 options (i) Graphite (C), (iii) Chrome alum and (iv) Silicon carbide (SiC). Instead of that quartz glass has a short-range order i.e. the repetitive pattern falls through a short distance only and the regular pattern is scattered and they are disarrayed in nature (resemblances that of a liquid) unlike quartz itself which is a crystalline solid has long-range order and have regular pattern arrangement.

questions.question_text: The tanks used by divers are filled with air diluted with
questions.question_html: The tanks used by divers are filled with air diluted with
questions.explanation: Size of Helium is small so does not causes “Bents” to divers when the dive back to surface. As it is less soluble.

questions.question_text: Which gas has a smell of rotten fish?
questions.question_html: Which gas has a smell of rotten fish?
questions.explanation: Phosphine smell like rotten fish due to the presence of substituted phosphine and diphosphine (P 2 H 4 ). Pure phosphine has no characteristic smell.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is employed as antityphoid drug?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is employed as antityphoid drug?
questions.explanation: Chlorine containing antibiotics, chloramphenicol, produced by soil microorganisms is very effective for the treatment of typhoid fever. Chloramphenicol is an antityphoid drug.

questions.question_text: Alcohols are easily dehydrated in the following order:
questions.question_html: Alcohols are easily dehydrated in the following order:
questions.explanation: The rate of dehydration is related to the ease of formation of Carbocation and the energy of the carbocation intermediate. Dehydration involves the formation of carbocation 1 st and since tertiary carbocation is more stable than secondary and primary therefore 3° is dehydrated first. The ease of formation of Carbocation is Tertiary>Secondary>Primary.

questions.question_text: Peptide linkage is present in:
questions.question_html: Peptide linkage is present in:
questions.explanation: Peptide linkage is present in proteins. It is a chemical bond formed between two molecules when the carboxyl group of one molecule reacts with the amino group of other molecules, releasing a molecule of water.

questions.question_text: The percentage of empty space in a body-centered cubic arrangement is ________.
questions.question_html: The percentage of empty space in a body-centered cubic arrangement is ________.
questions.explanation: Packing efficiency for body centred cubic is 68% which represents total filled space in the unit cells about 68%. Hence, empty space in a body centred arrangement is 100 - 68 = 32 %.

questions.question_text: An aqueous solution of methanol and water has vapour pressure
questions.question_html: An aqueous solution of methanol and water has vapour pressure
questions.explanation: They show positive deviation.

questions.question_text: Fluorine reacts with H 2 S to produce
questions.question_html: Fluorine reacts with H2S to produce
questions.explanation: 4F 2 + H 2 S {tex}\to{/tex} SF 6 + 2HF

questions.question_text: Which one of the following forms propane nitrile as the major product?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following forms propane nitrile as the major product?
questions.explanation: CH 3 CH 2 Br + KCN {tex} \to {/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CN + KBr

questions.question_text: Aldehydes react with Grignard reagent to produce______.
questions.question_html: Aldehydes react with Grignard reagent to produce______.
questions.explanation: The Grignard Reaction is the addition of an organomagnesium halide (Grignard reagent) to a ketone or aldehyde, to form tertiary or secondary alcohol, respectively. The reaction with formaldehyde leads to primary alcohol. Grignard Reagents are also used in the following important reactions: The addition of an excess of a Grignard reagent to an ester or lactone gives tertiary alcohol in which two alkyl groups are the same, and the addition of a Grignard reagent to a nitrile produces an unsymmetrical ketone via a metalloimine intermediate. RCHO + R 1 MgX {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCH(OH)R 1

questions.question_text: ___ are considered as essential food factors.
questions.question_html: ___  are considered as essential food factors.
questions.explanation: Certain organic compounds are required in small amounts in our diet but their deficiency causes specific diseases. These compounds are called vitamins. Most of the vitamins cannot be synthesised in our body but plants can synthesise almost all of them, so they are considered as essential food factors. However, the bacteria of the gut can produce some of the vitamins required by us. All the vitamins are generally available in our diet.

questions.question_text: Available chlorine is librated from bleaching powder when:
questions.question_html: Available chlorine is librated from bleaching powder when:

options.text: It is reacted with dilute acid
options.html: It is reacted with dilute acid

questions.correct_option_text: It is reacted with dilute acid
questions.explanation: Bleaching powder when reacts with dilute acids liberates chlorine. Ca(OCl) 2 + 4 HCl {tex}\to{/tex} CaCl 2 + H 2 O + 2Cl 2 {tex}\uparrow{/tex} The main constituent of bleaching powder is calcium hypochlorite which supplies chlorine with dilute acids.

questions.question_html: Which crystal system has {tex}a \ne b \ne c{/tex}?
questions.explanation: In orthorhombic crystal system have all different sizes.

options.text: it gains water due to osmosis.

options.html: it loses water due to reverse osmosis.

options.text: it gains water due to reverse osmosis.
options.html: it gains water due to reverse osmosis.

options.text: it loses water due to osmosis.
options.html: it loses water due to osmosis.

questions.correct_option_text: it loses water due to osmosis.
questions.explanation: Water starts moving out of mango (lower concentration) to the salt solution or brine (higher concentration) due to osmosis as a result of mango shrivel.

questions.question_text: Insulin is a protein which contains _______ amino acids
questions.question_html: Insulin is a protein which contains _______ amino acids
questions.explanation: Insulin contain 51 amino acids

questions.question_text: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.question_html: Which compound is used as the cooling liquid in refrigerators?
questions.explanation: Liquid NH 3 is used as a refrigerant. evaporation of a liquid needs heat energy. When liquid ammonia vapourises, it absorbs large quantities of heat without changing its temperature.

questions.question_text: Hydrocarbons having double the number of carbon atoms than present in the original alkyl halide are produced by using:
questions.question_html: Hydrocarbons having double the number of carbon atoms than present in the original alkyl halide are produced by using:
questions.explanation: The Wurtz reaction , named after Charles-Adolphe Wurtz , is a coupling reaction in organic chemistry, organometallic chemistry and recently inorganic main group polymers, whereby two alkyl halides are reacted with sodium metal in dry ethereal solution to form a higher alkane: 2R - X + 2Na {tex} \to {/tex} R - R + 2NaX

questions.question_text: In the preparation of compounds of Xe, Bartlett had taken {tex}\mathrm{O}_{2}^{+} \mathrm{Pt} \mathrm{F}_{6}^{-}{/tex} as a base compound. This is because
questions.question_html: In the preparation of compounds of Xe, Bartlett had taken {tex}\mathrm{O}_{2}^{+} \mathrm{Pt} \mathrm{F}_{6}^{-}{/tex} as a base compound. This is because

options.text: both O 2 and Xe have almost same ionisation enthalpy.
options.html: both O<sub>2</sub> and Xe have almost same ionisation enthalpy.

options.text: both O 2 and Xe have the same size.
options.html: both O<sub>2</sub> and Xe have the same size.

options.text: both Xe and O 2 are gases.
options.html: both Xe and O<sub>2</sub> are gases.

options.text: both O 2 and Xe have the same electron gain enthalpy.
options.html: both O<sub>2</sub> and Xe have the same electron gain enthalpy.

questions.correct_option_text: both O 2 and Xe have almost same ionisation enthalpy.
questions.explanation: Bartlett had taken O 2 Pt as a base compound because O 2 and Xe both have almost same ionization enthalpy i.e O ​​​​​​2 (1175KJmol -1 ) and Xe(1170KJmol -1 ). The ionization enthalpies of noble gases are the highest in their respective periods due to their stable electronic configurations.

questions.question_text: An example of globular protein is
questions.question_html: An example of globular protein is
questions.explanation: Globular proteins are formed when the chains of polypeptides coil around to give a spherical shape. These are usually soluble in water. For example albumin and insulin.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following compound is most acidic?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following compound is most acidic?

questions.question_text: The following diagram shows
questions.question_html: The following diagram shows

options.text: Schematic set up of a desalination plant
options.html: Schematic set up of a desalination plant

questions.correct_option_text: Schematic set up of a desalination plant
questions.explanation: The desalination process works on the principle of reverse osmosis. Reverse osmosis occurs when a pressure larger than the osmotic pressure is applied to the solution.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an amphoteric oxide?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is an amphoteric oxide?
questions.explanation: Higher oxidation state oxides are acidic while lower oxidation state oxides are basic. Intermediate oxidation state oxides are amphoteric. In other words, it behaves acidic with bases and as basic with acids. The oxidation state of Cr in Cr 2 O 3 is +3, Vanadium in V 2 O 5 is +5, Cl in Cl 2 O 7 is +7, and Sn in SnO 2 is +4.

questions.question_text: The best method for the conversion of an alcohol into an alkyl chloride is by treating the alcohol with:
questions.question_html: The best method for the conversion of an alcohol into an alkyl chloride is by treating the alcohol with:

options.text: Dry HCl in the presence of anhydrous ZnCl 2
options.html: Dry HCl in the presence of anhydrous ZnCl<sub>2</sub>

questions.explanation: The hydroxyl group of an alcohol is replaced by halogen on reaction with concentrated halogen acids, phosphorus halides, or thionyl chloride. Thionyl chloride (SOCl 2 ) is preferred because the other two products SO 2 and HCl are escapable gases. Hence, the reaction gives pure alkyl halides. ROH + SOCl 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCl + SO 2 (g) + HCl (g)

questions.question_text: Iodine molecules are held in the crystals lattice by ________.
questions.question_html: Iodine molecules are held in the crystals lattice by ________.
questions.explanation: Iodine molecule is a nonpolar covalent molecule. Thes molecules are electrically symmetrical and have no dipole moment. The molecules are in a crystal lattice of iodine are, therefore, attracted together by weak dispersed forces or weak London forces.

questions.question_text: Anisole can be prepared by the action of methyl iodide on sodium phenate. The reaction is called
questions.question_html: Anisole can be prepared by the action of methyl iodide on sodium phenate. The reaction is called
questions.explanation: The reaction of an alkyl halide with sodium alkoxide to give ether (alkoxy alkane), is known as Williamson's synthesis. In this reaction, an ether (anisole) is prepared by the action of alkyl halide (methyl iodide) on sodium alkoxide (sodium phenate), so it is an example of Williamson's synthesis.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statement is not true about amorphous solids?
questions.question_html: Which of the following statement is not true about amorphous solids?

options.text: Amorphous solids can be moulded by heating.
options.html: Amorphous solids can be moulded by heating.

options.text: On heating they may become crystalline at certain temperature.
options.html: On heating they may become crystalline at certain temperature.

options.text: They may become crystalline on keeping for long time.
options.html: They may become crystalline on keeping for long time.

questions.explanation: Amorphous solids are isotropic because they show thermal and optical properties, same in all directions while crystalline solid are anisotropic.

questions.question_text: Which condition is not satisfied by an ideal solution?
questions.question_html: Which condition is not satisfied by an ideal solution?
questions.explanation: For an ideal solution. Δ mixing H=0, Δ mixing V=0 and it should obey Raoult's law.

questions.question_text: Which of the following pairs of ions are isoelectronic and isostructural? {tex}\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}, \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{ClO}_{3}^{-}, \mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3}^{2-}, \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{ClO}_{3}^{-}, \mathrm{SO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Which of the following pairs of ions are isoelectronic and isostructural?

  1. {tex}\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}, \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}{/tex}
  2. {tex}\mathrm{ClO}_{3}^{-}, \mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex}
  3. {tex}\mathrm{SO}_{3}^{2-}, \mathrm{NO}_{3}^{-}{/tex}
  4. {tex}\mathrm{ClO}_{3}^{-}, \mathrm{SO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}\mathrm{CO}_{3}^{2-}{/tex} , Number of electrons = 6 + 3(8) + 2 = 32 According to VSEPR theory it is trigonal planar in shape. NO 3- , Number of electrons = 7 + 3(8) + 1 = 32 According to VSEPR theory it is trigonal planar in shape. ClO 3 - , Number of electrons = 17 + 3(8) + 1 = 42 According to VSEPR theory, it is trigonal pyramidal in shape. SO 3 2- , Number of electrons = 16 + 3(8) + 2 = 42 According to VSEPR theory, it is trigonal pyramidal in shape. Thus, CO 3 2- , NO 3 - and CIO 3 - , SO 3 2- are isoelectronic

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC names of the following compound: CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 CH(Br)CH(CH 3 )CH 2 CHO
questions.question_html: Give IUPAC names of the following compound: CH3CH2CH2CH(Br)CH(CH3)CH2CHO
questions.explanation: The IUPAC of the above compound will be more clear if we open up the structure: The structure of the above compounds is (longest chain contain 7 carbon) Hence the IUPAC name of the above compound is 4-Bromo-3-methylheptanal.

questions.question_text: The oxidation state of central atom in the anion of compound NaH 2 PO 2 will be ________.
questions.question_html: The oxidation state of central atom in the anion of compound NaH2PO2 will be ________.
questions.explanation: Oxidation state of P in NaH ​​​​2 PO 2 is (+ 1) + (+1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) + x + (-2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) = 0 x = + 1

questions.question_text: Zwitter ionic form of amino acids behave like
questions.question_html: Zwitter ionic form of amino acids behave like
questions.explanation: Amino acids behave like salts rather than simple amines or carboxylic acids. This behaviour is due to the presence of both acidic (carboxyl group) and basic (amino group) groups in the same molecule. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges.

questions.question_text: The iodine-containing hormone produced by our body is:
questions.question_html: The iodine-containing hormone produced by our body is:
questions.explanation: Our body produces iodine-containing hormone, thyroxine, the deficiency of which causes a disease called a goiter.

questions.question_text: Anisole is called
questions.question_html: Anisole is called
questions.explanation: Anisole, or methoxybenzene, is an organic compound with the formula CH 3 OC 6 H 5 . It is a colorless liquid with a smell reminiscent of anise seed, and in fact many of its derivatives are found in natural and artificial fragrances.

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: All the elements of group 16 have ns 2 np 4 configuration in their outermost shell. Therefore, the atoms of these elements try to gain or share two electrons to achieve noble gas configuration. Sulphur and other elements of group 16 are less electronegative than oxygen, so, they cannot accept electrons easily. By sharing of two electrons with other elements, these elements acquire ns 2 np 6 configuration and exhibit +2 oxidation state. Except for oxygen, group 16 elements have vacant d-orbitals in their valence shell to which electrons can be promoted from p - and s-orbitals of the same shell. As a result, they can show +4 and +6 oxidation states also. Like sulphur, oxygen is not able to show +4 and +6 oxidation states because
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

All the elements of group 16 have ns2 np4 configuration in their outermost shell. Therefore, the atoms of these elements try to gain or share two electrons to achieve noble gas configuration. Sulphur and other elements of group 16 are less electronegative than oxygen, so, they cannot accept electrons easily. By sharing of two electrons with other elements, these elements acquire ns2 np6 configuration and exhibit +2 oxidation state. Except for oxygen, group 16 elements have vacant d-orbitals in their valence shell to which electrons can be promoted from p - and s-orbitals of the same shell. As a result, they can show +4 and +6 oxidation states also.
 


Like sulphur, oxygen is not able to show +4 and +6 oxidation states because

options.text: oxygen is a gas while sulphur is a solid
options.html: oxygen is a gas while sulphur is a solid

options.text: oxygen has no d-orbitals in its valence shell
options.html: oxygen has no d-orbitals in its valence shell

options.text: oxygen has high electron affinity as compared to sulphur
options.html: oxygen has high electron affinity as compared to sulphur

options.text: sulphur has high ionisation enthalpy as compared to oxygen
options.html: sulphur has high ionisation enthalpy as compared to oxygen

questions.correct_option_text: oxygen has no d-orbitals in its valence shell
questions.explanation: sulphur has high ionisation enthalpy as compared to oxygen

Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

All the elements of group 16 have ns2 np4 configuration in their outermost shell. Therefore, the atoms of these elements try to gain or share two electrons to achieve noble gas configuration. Sulphur and other elements of group 16 are less electronegative than oxygen, so, they cannot accept electrons easily. By sharing of two electrons with other elements, these elements acquire ns2 np6 configuration and exhibit +2 oxidation state. Except for oxygen, group 16 elements have vacant d-orbitals in their valence shell to which electrons can be promoted from p - and s-orbitals of the same shell. As a result, they can show +4 and +6 oxidation states also.
 


Compounds of sulphur with +4 oxidation state acts as a/an

options.text: both oxidising as well as reducing agent
options.html: both oxidising as well as reducing agent

questions.correct_option_text: both oxidising as well as reducing agent
questions.explanation: In SO 2 , sulphur having +4 oxidation state, so it can lose its two more electrons to attain +6 oxidation state. It can gain electrons to attain its lowest oxidation state of -2. Therefore, it can behave as both a reducing and oxidising agent.

Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

All the elements of group 16 have ns2 np4 configuration in their outermost shell. Therefore, the atoms of these elements try to gain or share two electrons to achieve noble gas configuration. Sulphur and other elements of group 16 are less electronegative than oxygen, so, they cannot accept electrons easily. By sharing of two electrons with other elements, these elements acquire ns2 np6 configuration and exhibit +2 oxidation state. Except for oxygen, group 16 elements have vacant d-orbitals in their valence shell to which electrons can be promoted from p - and s-orbitals of the same shell. As a result, they can show +4 and +6 oxidation states also.
 


Oxidation state of sulphur in Na2S4O6 is
questions.explanation: Na 2 S 4 O 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(+1) + 4x + 6(-2) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
